entries.text: keep going — break the task into the next small step
continue. Run the tests if you're at a checkpoint.
don't pause. Commit what's working and keep moving.
what's the next subtask? Name it and start it.
if you're stuck, say what's blocking — out loud is clearer.
small step forward. Don't try to finish in one leap.
check git status, then keep going from the last good point.
you've got momentum. Don't break it looking for perfection.
ship working code now; refine in the next commit.
re-read the last task description. What haven't you done?
pick the smallest remaining thing and finish it.
run the tests. Green? Keep going. Red? Fix first.
you're closer than you think. Keep pushing.
don't start something new — finish the current thing.
save progress with a commit, then continue.
what's the acceptance criterion? Are you meeting it?
keep it simple. No new abstractions unless needed.
walk through the edge cases. Did you cover empty input?
what would you do if you had 5 more minutes? Do that.
stop narrating. Execute.
the test is the spec. Does it pass?
check the file you last edited. Is it saved?
one clear step, then the next. Don't jump around.
if the fix is 3 lines, write 3 lines. Don't refactor.
read the error message carefully, then act.
you have the answer already. Just write it.
commit the failing test first, then make it pass.
re-read the requirement. Is this actually what was asked?
don't add features the task didn't ask for.
is there a simpler version of this that would work?
finish the current PR before starting a new one.
the plan is in the spec. Follow it task-by-task.
short commits, clear messages. Keep going.
a failing test is progress. Green it and move on.
the next step is obvious once you look at the code.
don't polish. Get it working first.
what does the test expect? Match that shape exactly.
trust the scaffolding. Don't rebuild what's already there.
keep the diff small. Easier to review, easier to revert.
what's left on the checklist? Do the next box.
one file at a time. Finish, save, move.
don't over-engineer. YAGNI.
are the tests green? If yes, ship it.
what's the 20% that gets 80% done right now?
the blocker is almost always smaller than it feels.
stop reading code. Start writing.
if it runs, commit it. Then improve.
don't second-guess a passing test.
the feature isn't done until it's dogfooded.
you're 2 minutes from finished. Keep going.
